title: 2026 Oscars Best Supporting Actor Predictions
text: The supporting actor race has emerged as one of the season's most unpredictable categories. According to the article, five worthy contenders are in competition. Stellan Skarsgård (Sentimental Value) was initially considered unbeatable after winning the Golden Globe but faced a major red flag when excluded from SAG nominations, disrupting his frontrunner status. Jacob Elordi (Frankenstein) gained momentum with a Critics' Choice Award win, while Benicio Del Toro (One Battle After Another) doubled Skarsgård's critics' wins, further fragmenting the race. Sean Penn (One Battle After Another) won both SAG and BAFTA awards and is widely viewed as the likely victor, though some factors suggest potential vulnerabilities. Delroy Lindo (Sinners), a 73-year-old veteran, received a surprise nomination despite being snubbed by every major precursor. Davis notes that only three modern actors have won without SAG nominations: Marcia Gay Harden, Regina King, and Christoph Waltz, with Harden being the only precedent matching Lindo's lack of precursor recognition. The article suggests Lindo could become the modern Marcia Gay Harden, especially as Sinners surges toward a potential best picture nomination.
